title: Microsoft blames unexpected Windows driver updates on caching issue
summary: On Wednesday, Microsoft fixed an issue that caused some Windows devices to install driver updates without notice despite policies configured to prevent auto-updates. [...]

title: Police dismantles fake ID marketplace used by migrant smugglers
summary: French and Spanish authorities took down an online marketplace selling fake identity documents to migrant smuggling rings operating within the European Union. [...]

title: Cisco warns of critical Unified CM flaw with PoC exploit code
summary: Cisco has released security updates to patch a critical-severity Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) flaw that allows attackers to gain root privileges. [...]

title: Chinese hackers use new Atlas RAT malware in European cyberattacks
summary: A Chinese-speaking cybercrime group has expanded its targeting to the European space, deploying previously undocumented malware and the Atlas backdoor. [...]

title: U.S. sanctions Nobitex crypto exchange used by Iranian ransomware actors
summary: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has announced sanctions against Nobitex, Iran's largest cryptocurrency exchange, for facilitating payments related to terrorist activities. [...]

title: CISA warns of cyberattacks targeting fuel tank monitoring systems
summary: CISA, the FBI, the NSA, the Department of Energy, and other US government partners are warning that hackers are targeting internet-exposed automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems used to monitor fuel and liquid storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors. [...]

title: New 'HTTP/2 Bomb' DoS attack crashes web servers in under a minute
summary: A new denial-of-service (DoS) attack dubbed HTTP/2 Bomb can be launched from a single machine to take down web servers within seconds. [...]

title: CISA warns of active attacks exploiting Android, Linux bugs
summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel and Android operating system. [...]

title: What 345 Days of Untested Exposure Looks Like at a Bank
summary: A two-week penetration test can leave roughly 345 days of real-world exposure unvalidated. Sprocket Security explores why continuous testing is becoming critical as attack surfaces constantly change. [...]

title: Acer working to patch max severity zero-days in Wave 7 routers
summary: Acer is working to address two maximum-severity zero-day vulnerabilities affecting its Wave 7 mesh routers. [...]

title: Police dismantles 9 crime groups in illegal streaming crackdown
summary: European and international law enforcement agencies have dismantled nine organized crime groups and arrested 29 suspects in a major crackdown on illegal streaming operations. [...]

title: Google adds Android protection against AI deepfake scam calls
summary: Google is introducing a new Android security feature that will detect and flag phone calls in which scammers use artificial intelligence to impersonate a user's personal contacts. [...]

title: VS Code zero-day lets hackers steal GitHub tokens in one click
summary: A security researcher has released exploit code for a Visual Studio Code (VS Code) zero-day vulnerability that allows attackers to steal GitHub authentication tokens by tricking users into clicking a link. [...]

title: Microsoft's Coreutils project brings Linux commands to Windows
summary: Microsoft announced today at its Build 2026 developer conference the release of Coreutils for Windows, bringing many commonly used Linux command-line utilities to Windows as native applications. [...]

title: OpenAI upgrades GPT-5.5, as it plans to retire legacy ChatGPT models
summary: OpenAI says it's rolling out a new update that improves the existing GPT-5.5 Instant model, and this move comes ahead of the scheduled retirement of multiple legacy models, including o3. [...]
